title: Cyclobalanus diepenhorstii

The exact classification of Cyclobalanus diepenhorstii can be complex and involves various botanical categories. It is typically classified as a member of the genus Cyclobalanus, within the family Fagaceae, which includes other oak-like trees. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.

intro: Cyclobalanus diepenhorstii, also known as Cyclobalanopsis glauca, is a species of evergreen tree native to China and Southeast Asia, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cyclobalanus diepenhorstii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Cyclobalanus diepenhorstii is commonly prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in the formulation of capsules, tablets, and injections to treat various health conditions.
